---
title: "Coastal Graphic (Drasteria graphica)"
slug: "drasteria-graphica"
group: "Insecta"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623052794/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Coastal Graphic"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Animal"
tax_class: "Insecta"
tax_order: "Lepidoptera"
tax_family: "Erebidae"
categories: ["Animal", "Insecta", "Lepidoptera", "Erebidae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623052794/medium.jpgg" alt="Coastal Graphic" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/coreytcallaghan">coreytcallaghan</a> (CC BY-NC 4.0) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342171590">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Insecta
-   **Order:** Lepidoptera
-   **Family:** Erebidae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=218723)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon in Florida.

## Natural history notes

Medium-sized moth characterized by mottled gray forewings that provide excellent camouflage against sand and bark, with more boldly patterned hindwings that are usually hidden at rest. In Florida, it is found primarily in coastal dune habitats where its larvae feed on beach heather (Hudsonia spp.). Adults are typically active in the warmer months and are attracted to light at night.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/218723-Drasteria-graphica)
-   [Local profile](https://www.vararespecies.org/specie/Drasteria%20graphica)
